title: Fix the chat websocket protocol and message history
description:
# Issue #81 - Chat client crashes parsing replies; history is always empty

The web chat client calls JSON.parse on every websocket frame, but /ws/chat replies with plain strings like 'alice: hi'. The history panel (GET /api/messages) is always empty because messages are never recorded, and a client disconnect raises an unhandled exception in the logs.

**Protocol:** every received frame {user, text} must be answered with a JSON frame {user, text, timestamp} and appended to the in-memory MESSAGES history.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- websocket replies are JSON with user, text and a numeric timestamp
- every message appears in GET /api/messages in order
- client disconnects are handled cleanly (no unhandled WebSocketDisconnect)
optimal_hint: Ideal: build message = {user, text, timestamp: time.time()}, MESSAGES.append(message), await websocket.send_json(message), and wrap the receive loop in try/except WebSocketDisconnect. Watch for students who json.dumps into send_text (acceptable) vs send_json (cleaner).
